title: QUIC RFC 9001 Known Stateless Reset Loop Suppression Work Item
summary: Implement and prove the retained-route known-token loop-suppression matrix cell after AEAD-limit terminal discard.
planned_changes:
- Add an explicit non-emitted disposition for retained-route triggers that are known stateless resets.
- Suppress emission before rate-budget reservation when a reset-shaped retained-route trigger carries the retained token for the same remote address.
- Keep different trailing tokens eligible for the existing retained-route response path when the route, remote endpoint, loop-prevention, and rate gates pass.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for direct endpoint suppression, different-token retained-route emission, non-reset-shaped exclusion, endpoint-host suppression, and deterministic fuzz coverage over route, remote endpoint, and token-match combinations.
- Add a permanent stateless-reset benchmark case for the known-reset suppression branch.
verification_plan: Run the focused REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008 requirement-home tests, keep the retained-route and formatting matrix guards green, refresh QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks in Dry mode, render and check affected SpecTrace Markdown from JSON, and finish with git diff --check.
completion_notes: Completed for the known-token loop-suppression cell only. Broader non-retained and untraced post-stop response behavior remains blocked in REQUIREMENT-GAPS.
